titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Nelson Morpurgo Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 6255744.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.abbr: beinecke manuscript unit archival processing manual

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday February 27, 2021 at 04:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of manuscripts, correspondence and other papers pertaining to Morpurgo's literary career, including manuscripts of Il fuoco delle piramidi (1923) and several poems.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Ongoing collection of documents acquired by gift and purchase from various sources. Source information is recorded on the folders. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Nelson Morpurgo Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Nelson Morpurgo Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This finding aid is updated periodically to account for new materials that have been added to the collection. The date of the most recent update is noted on the title page. For information on material that may have been acquired for the collection since the last update, please consult the Public Service Desk.

bioghist.head: NELSON MORPURGO, 1899-1978
bioghist.p: Nelson Morpurgo was born in Cairo in 1899, studied law and jurisprudence in France and Italy, and began the practice of law in Cairo in 1933. After World War II he became legal advisor to the Italian Consulate in Egypt, and later was a founder of and advisor to the Italian Chamber of Commerce in Egypt.

Morpurgo is best known as a Futurist author and as a friend of Filippo Marinetti. His publications include Morfina (1921); Il fuoco del Piramidi (1923); and Per le mi donne (1932). After his retirement in Rimini, Morpurgo organized a conference on Futurism and participated in the celebration of the centenary of Marinetti's birth in 1976. He died in Rimini on October 11, 1978.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
This finding aid encompasses accessions of single manuscripts and small groups of manuscripts and other papers, by or pertaining to Nelson Morpurgo, acquired by the library from a variety of sources. Series I, Accession of May 2001
, contains typescripts of poems by Morpurgo and a photograph. Materials added to the collection since that time are located in Series II, Additions since July 2002
, listed in the order in which they were added.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript pages of poems, corrected and marked for printer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: AMS of poem in two colors of ink.

did.unittitle: Photograph (postcard) of Nelson Morpurgo attending a Fascist march in Milan

descriptivenote.p: Photograph (postcard) of Nelson Morpurgo attending a Fascist march in Milan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Purchased on the Edwin J. Beinecke Fund and through a gift from the Sonia Raiziss Giop Fund through the Library Associates, 2003

did.unittitle: TL carbon to the editor of La Bourse Egyptiennes ; Cairo
TL carbon to the editor of Varietés ; Cairo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 typescript carbons, one corrected and one incorporating the corrections

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript with holograph additions, signed and dated

did.unittitle: Menu for a "Futurist" meal at "Celestino" in Cairo

descriptivenote.p: Menu for a "Futurist" meal at "Celestino" in Cairo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Holograph, illustrated, signed by several of the participants, including Morpurgo, Gino Grossi, Umberto Spallanzani, and Umberto Ricci

did.unittitle: Photograph of group of men and women

descriptivenote.p: Photograph of group of men and women

did.unittitle: Photograph group portrait of Italian men in Cairo

descriptivenote.p: Photograph group portrait of Italian men in Cairo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Morpurgo; signatures of some members on verso

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains article by Jean Moscatelli about Morpurgo, p. 11

did.unittitle: Printed: tickets for a performance of Morfina!

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With printed notices of performance on verso

did.unittitle: Printed: legal file holder pertaining to the firm of C. Morpurgo and M. Castro, Cairo
Printed: empty envelope decorated with engraving of skull and motto, "Non morior"
